last_item: Band-2.5 (resource & config safety): APPLY host-sized tuning at provision — Manager.ApplyTuning persists RecommendTuning settings via ALTER SYSTEM, idempotent vs pg_settings, restart-requiring ones funneled through restartWithRollback; wired into Provision (replaces iter-28 'surfaced, not applied').
last_result: done (iter 29) — added Manager.ApplyTuning(ctx, rec) in internal/pg/tuning_apply.go: persists the five core settings via ALTER SYSTEM as the postgres OS superuser; idempotent by reading pg_settings and normalising each value to bytes via the unit column (settingUnitBytes handles 8kB/kB/MB/GB + unit-less), skipping settings already at the wanted value (re-provision = no-op, no needless restart). Restart-requiring settings (shared_buffers, max_connections) funnel through the iter-26 restartWithRollback primitive (snapshot postgresql.auto.conf BEFORE write; on failed restart restore last-known-good so PG never left down -> CodeSafety); reloadable-only changes use pg_reload_conf with no restart. Integer GUCs (max_connections) written UNquoted, memory settings quoted (alterValue; reviewer-caught — a quoted integer GUC is fragile). Wired into Provision replacing iter-28's surfaced-only block: applies Mixed-profile host-sized defaults and GRACEFULLY tolerates a CodeSafety rollback (m.log.Warn + result data tuning:'rejected (rolled back...)', carries on — an oversized default must not fail the whole provision). Result keeps recommended_tuning, adds tuning status. Added a detectTuning seam field on Manager (nil->detectHostTuning) so provision tests pin RAM/CPU deterministically (host RAM would otherwise make them flaky). Tests: ApplyTuning no-op/reload-only/restart/restart+reloadable-together/rollback-on-restart-fail/missing-setting/no-runner + settingUnitBytes table + alterValue quoting; TestProvision happy-path + idempotent updated to pin tuning with NATIVE pg_settings units (8kB blocks, kB) + assert the no-op path. Go gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build, sandbox-disabled per snap); web untouched. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): fixed integer-GUC quoting + the synthetic-MB test-unit gap, added the mixed restart+reload test.
notes: BAND 1.5 + BAND 2 COMPLETE. IN BAND 2.5 (resource & config safety). DONE iter 29: APPLY host-sized tuning at provision — Manager.ApplyTuning (internal/pg/tuning_apply.go) persists RecommendTuning's five settings via ALTER SYSTEM, idempotent vs pg_settings (bytes-normalised through the unit col), restart-requiring (shared_buffers/max_connections) funneled through restartWithRollback, reloadable via pg_reload_conf. Wired into Provision (replaces iter-28 'surfaced, not applied'); a CodeSafety rollback is tolerated gracefully (provision still succeeds on prior good config). detectTuning seam added for deterministic provision tests. Fully unit-tested. THE TWO NEW TOP BAND-2.5 ITEMS are the apply follow-ups: (a) a real-PG //go:build integration test proving settings land in pg_settings, a 2nd run no-ops, and an oversized shared_buffers rolls back with PG up; (b) the UI surface showing the applied defaults (Provision already returns recommended_tuning + tuning status) + the workload-profile override labeled by effect. ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write) — ApplyTuning is the worked example. DONE iter 28: host-sized tuning PURE SIZING FUNCTION — RecommendTuning(memoryMB, cpuCount, profile) in internal/pg/tuning.go ports the sm tuning.py math (DEFAULTS.md) for shared_buffers/effective_cache_size/work_mem/maintenance_work_mem/max_connections; WorkloadProfile selectable, empty->Mixed default, unknown->ValidationError; total/panic-free on degenerate input. Tested vs sample RAM 1GB-128GB x all 3 profiles + clamp/ordering/degenerate/parse/SettingsMap. DONE iter 27: connection-saturation alert CRITICAL tier connections-critical (pg.connections_percent >= 95%, For 1m, Cooldown 15m, Critical) escalating the existing connections-near-max WARNING (85%); rule-only, metric already real (sampler.go pg_stat_activity / max_connections), ID-based seed auto-adds on upgrade; TestConnectionSaturationTiers proves both tiers fire non-vacuously. DONE iter 26: SELF-HEALING config primitive (internal/pg/safeconfig.go): snapshotAutoConf captures <data_directory>/postgresql.auto.conf BEFORE a restart-requiring change (fails closed if unreadable); restartWithRollback restarts PG and on non-zero `systemctl restart` exit restores the snapshot via `tee` as the postgres user (keeps owner+0600; works while PG is down, unlike ALTER SYSTEM RESET) + restarts again so PG is never left down -> CodeSafety ('rolled back; PG running') or CodeInternal ('PG down'+hint). ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write) — this is exactly what the new APPLY item above must do. DONE iter 25: disk-headroom-low early-warning rule (WARNING 80% host.disk_percent) below critical disk-almost-full (90%). KEY: adding a disk/conn alert is usually RULE-ONLY — Snapshot already has the fields, alert/metrics.go metricValue computes the percent, and background.go's seedDefaultAlertRules inserts only missing IDs (idempotent) so a new DefaultRules() entry auto-seeds on upgrade. Test pattern: extend TestDefaultRules want-list + a focused engine test; background_test asserts len(DefaultRules()) dynamically. --- Alert plumbing: Collector.SampleOnce -> Engine.Evaluate -> dispatchAlertEvents (background.go); metricValue (alert/metrics.go) reads Snapshot fields; go is a snap -> run go/psql/pg_ctl sandbox-disabled; empty $TMPDIR in some shells -> use /tmp. --- HISTORICAL: band 1.5 (backups proven-restorable, S3 nudge, restore-test verify+deep, scheduled jobs) and band 2 (web silent-failure audit, idempotent provisioning, query-box auto-LIMIT+statement_timeout enforced) all COMPLETE. See git log.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
